A.rationale: Correct O(n) seen-set, order preserved, tests pass. Accurately notes original masked unhashables silently while new raises loudly.

B.rationale: Correct fix, but false claim: old list-membership uses == not __hash__, so original did NOT raise on unhashables. Technical error.

C.rationale: Correct O(n) seen-set, order preserved, tests pass. Tight, accurate unhashable caveat without overclaiming about the original's behavior.

D.rationale: Correct fix; rightly notes original didn't raise on unhashables, new does. Slightly verbose and minor imprecision on 'non-equal'.

notes: A, C, D all technically correct; C is tightest while accurate. B asserts the old list-membership used __hash__ — false, it uses ==, so it loses a correctness point.
